Exploring VR: Technology and Immersive Experiences

Virtual reality gaming transports players into immersive digital worlds through advanced hardware and content. While it offers transformative potential, challenges such as adoption rates, comfort, and content availability exist, posing questions about VR's future in mainstream gaming.

Modern VR hardware spans from standalone headsets to PC-tethered devices offering varying price-performance trade-offs. Meta Quest 3 dominates standalone VR through accessible pricing ($500), wireless freedom, and capable processing that enables quality experiences without external hardware. It boasts a massive content library built across Quest platform iterations, though graphical fidelity and processing power cannot match PC VR. Voice Agents Pick Up Pace As Customer Service Centers Embrace AI

ByDavid Prosser,

Contributor.

Follow Author
May 12, 2026, 08:30am EDT
Voice agents are expected to increasingly supplant human customer service representatives
GETTY IMAGES

Could you tell the difference between a human customer assistant and a tool powered by artificial intelligence (AI)? Vapi, which is today announcing it has raised $50 million of Series B funding, says its voice technology is so good that many users won’t notice they’re talking to such a tool.

Companies are increasingly pinning their hopes on such technology as they battle to improve customer satisfaction – as measured by metrics such as CSAT scores – that have continued to fall despite significant investment in a range of tools in recent years. Many customers report frustration with automated call handling services, call center delays and unwieldy chatbots; Vapi says its voice agents can overcome such issues.

DUBLIN--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The "Europe Data Center Colocation Market - Industry Outlook & Forecast 2025-2030" report has been added to ResearchAndMarkets.com's offering.

The Europe Data Center Colocation Market was valued at USD 9.45 Billion in 2024, and is projected to reach USD 35.73 Billion by 2030, rising at a CAGR of 24.82%.

The Europe data center colocation market is expected to witness cumulative investments of approximately $144.03 billion, of which, the Western Europe is slated to account for around $101.51 billion, representing around 70.48% of the overall investment share in the European region, followed by Nordics that contributed for approximately 20.60% of Europe data center colocation investments, and Central and Eastern European region contributed for around 8.92% of the investments in the region.

The attack dominating financial services doesn't steal passwords. It resets MFA and steals the token.
Louis Columbus
12:34 pm, PT, May 26, 2026

VentureBeat created with Imagen

The attacker who hit the most financial services organizations over the past 12 months never phished a password. They called an IT support line, convinced an employee to reset their MFA, and registered their own device on the network.

CrowdStrike’s 2026 Financial Services Threat Landscape Report, released this month and covering activity from April 2025 through March 2026, identified Mutant Spider as the single most active threat to the financial services sector. The group’s primary technique was voice phishing over Microsoft Teams. Operators impersonated internal IT support, convinced employees to reset their credentials and multifactor authentication, then registered their own devices on corporate networks. The security control worked exactly as designed — and that was the problem.

Keep Watching
